title: Marketing Analytics
text:
Below are selected projects that highlight my experience applying marketing analytics to real-world challenges. These projects reflect my ability to combine statistical tools, campaign data, and strategic thinking to inform decisions and drive results.
Whether developing dashboards, analyzing campaign performance, or uncovering insights through SEO and web analytics, each project is rooted in turning complex marketing data into clear, actionable strategies.

title: Marketing Analytics
text:
What You’ll Find

Campaign Performance Analysis: Evaluating the effectiveness of digital marketing efforts using data.
Data Visualization: Turning raw datasets into intuitive dashboards and reports.
Marketing Mix & Attribution: Exploring how different channels contribute to success.
Customer & Market Insights: Using analytics to better understand user behavior and optimize experiences.

title: Digital Marketing
text: Below are selected projects that showcase my work in strategic marketing, campaign development, and brand management. These examples highlight how I apply leadership, data-informed decision making, and creative direction to drive marketing success.

title: Digital Marketing
section: Project Title 1: Digital Marketing Strategy for Trauma Resource Institute (TRI)
text:
Project Title 1: Digital Marketing Strategy for Trauma Resource Institute (TRI)
This project is based on secondary data from the Trauma Resource Institute (TRI), including its website, mobile application (iChill), social media platforms, and industry competitor analysis. Additional insights were derived from digital marketing tools such as Google Ads Keyword Planner and market research.
Managerial Problem
TRI aims to increase brand awareness, improve engagement, and boost enrollment in its training programs (TRM and CRM). However, the organization has limited digital marketing presence, underutilized channels, and low conversion from its existing platforms, including its website and mobile application.
Target Audience
This project is intended for marketing managers, executives, and stakeholders responsible for digital growth and outreach. The target market includes mental health professionals, community leaders, individuals seeking trauma support, and global underserved populations.
Research Questions / Objectives

How can TRI improve its digital marketing strategy to increase visibility and engagement?
Which digital channels (Google Ads, social media, mobile app) are most effective?
How can TRI improve conversion rates for training programs?
How can digital campaigns be optimized to target specific audience segments?
Expected Impact on the Business

This project helps TRI improve its digital presence, attract more users, and increase enrollment in training programs. By optimizing digital channels and targeting the right audience, the organization can enhance engagement and expand its global reach.
Methods to Be Used
This project applies digital marketing analysis techniques including SWOT analysis, customer segmentation, competitor analysis, and campaign strategy development. It also includes keyword research, targeting strategy, and performance optimization planning using Google Ads.
Findings / Recommendations
The analysis identified key gaps in TRI’s digital marketing efforts, including lack of active Google Ads campaigns, low social media growth, and underutilization of the mobile app for conversions.
Key recommendations include:

Leveraging the $10,000 Google Ads Grant to run targeted campaigns
Improving SEO and keyword targeting strategies
Enhancing mobile app design to promote training programs
Increasing social media engagement through short-form content
Aligning messaging across all digital channels for consistency

title: Digital Marketing
section: Project Title 2: Ontario International Airport: Measuring Community Engagement ROI
text:
Project Title 2: Ontario International Airport: Measuring Community Engagement ROI
This project built a geotargeting and channel attribution framework for Ontario International Airport, integrating community event survey data with airport WiFi behavioral records to identify high-priority markets, rank media channels by conversion performance, and profile travelers for targeted post-event campaigns. The result is a repeatable, data-driven targeting model that connects community engagement directly to passenger behavior.
Data and findings protected under NDA.
Tools used:

ArcGIS Pro
R
Dedoose: Themeatic Analysis
Microsoft Excel

The Business Problem
Ontario International Airport competes directly with LAX, SNA, BUR, and SAN for Inland Empire passengers. Despite running hundreds of community engagement events annually, ONT had no way to answer three critical questions: - Which geographic markets are worth targeting after an event? - Which media channels are converting engagement into flight intent? - How should post-event campaigns be structured to reach the right people with the right message?
Our Approach
We integrated two independent datasets — a large-scale community event survey capturing post-event flight intent, media channel exposure, and residential ZIP codes, and airport WiFi login records from actual ONT passengers. Cross-validating stated survey intent against real passenger behavior is what separates this framework from a standard survey analysis. Six analytical methods were applied across the following strategic questions:

Geo-Scoring Model
WiFi Behavioral Cross-Validation
Channel-Level Attribution
Multi-Channel Synergy Analysis
Traveler Segment Profiling
Thematic Analysis

What the Framework Delivers
The output is a repeatable targeting and attribution infrastructure not a one-time analysis that ONT’s team can apply to every future engagement cycle:

A tiered ZIP code map directing post-event geofencing and email spend toward highest-conversion markets
A channel performance ranking providing data-backed rationale for media budget reallocation
A segment-differentiated creative strategy tailored to business vs. leisure vs visiting traveler profiles
A multi-channel sequencing architecture specifying the channel combinations associated with above-average intent conversion

Capabilities Demonstrated

Geo-scoring model design
GIS spatial mapping
Channel attribution modeling
Behavioral data cross-validation
Multi-channel synergy analysis
Traveler segmentation
Thematic analysis
Strategic recommendation development

title: Campaign Insights Dashboard
text:

This project involved building a dashboard to track and visualize marketing campaign performance. Metrics like click-through rate, conversion rate, and ROI were tracked across channels using R and Tableau.

title: Professional Summary
Hi, I’m Bansi Khachar
text:
About Me
I’ve always been more interested in the ‘why’ behind the numbers than the numbers themselves. What makes someone click? What makes them leave? What makes a campaign land — or fall flat? Those questions are what pulled me into marketing analytics, and they’re still what drive me every day.
I grew up in Ahmedabad, India, studied business and marketing, then moved to California to pursue a MS in Digital Marketing and Analytics at Cal Poly Pomona. That journey — across continents, cultures, and disciplines — gave me something that data alone can’t teach: a genuine appreciation for how differently people think, behave, and make decisions.
Today, I work at the intersection of data and human behavior. Whether I’m analyzing funnel drop-offs in Google Analytics, building attribution models with SQL and BigQuery, or designing surveys that ask the right questions — I’m always asking the same thing: why does this number look the way it does? And more importantly — what do we do about it?
When I’m not deep in dashboards, you’ll find me traveling — exploring new cities across the US and internationally, absorbing new cultures and perspectives. It keeps me grounded in something marketing can’t afford to forget: real people live very different lives, and the best strategies are built with that in mind.
